header.title: Your hunger solution
header.description: Can’t decide what to eat? Let us pick one for you!

error.general: An error occurred, please try again.

contact_us.title: Contact Us
contact_us.paragraph_1: This is a personal project — designed, built, and maintained by a couple people. The goal is simple: help anyone who’s ever wondered “what do I eat this lunch?” — whether you’re a local or a traveler exploring Thai cuisine for the first time and feeling a little overwhelmed by all the delicious options.
contact_us.paragraph_2: All the food info on this site was put together by hand. Some things might be a little off — please don’t rage-quit just yet! Drop us a message and we’ll sort it out.
contact_us.paragraph_3: Got questions, suggestions, or just want to say hi? Reach out at:
contact_us.paragraph_4: We’re genuinely happy to be part of your food journey. 🍜
contact_us.paragraph_5: Eat well, stay healthy, and may every meal be a good one!

about.title: About KinRaiDee
about.intro_paragraph_1: KinRaiDee (กินไรดี, literally “what should I eat?”) is a free food-decision helper built for one of life's most relatable struggles: standing in front of a menu — or a fridge — with absolutely no idea what to order. Instead of scrolling endlessly, you tell us a little about what you're in the mood for, and we suggest a dish for you.
about.intro_paragraph_2: We currently feature over 250 savory dishes and more than 150 desserts, spanning Thai regional cuisine (Central, Northern, Esan, and Southern) as well as Chinese, Korean, Japanese, Vietnamese, and Western favorites. You can filter by cuisine, region, spice level, and dietary needs such as healthy or soft-diet options.
about.how_paragraph_1: Pick whether you're craving something savory or sweet, optionally narrow it down by category, region, or cuisine, and hit the button. We'll randomly pick a dish that matches your filters. There's also a “Dish of the day” that refreshes daily if you'd rather leave it entirely to fate.
about.how_paragraph_2: Every dish in our database is curated and described by hand. We are constantly expanding the collection and refining the information so that locals and travelers alike can discover something new to eat.
about.who_paragraph_1: KinRaiDee is an independent passion project, designed, built, and maintained by a small team of food lovers. We are not affiliated with any restaurant and we do not receive payment for the recommendations we share — we simply want good food to be easier to find.
about.who_paragraph_2: Have a suggestion, a correction, or a restaurant we should try? We'd genuinely love to hear from you on our contact page.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains how KinRaiDee (“we”, “us”, or “our”) handles information when you visit kinraidee.info (the “Site”). We are committed to protecting your privacy and being transparent about the limited data involved in running this Site.
privacy.section_data_paragraph: KinRaiDee does not require you to create an account, and we do not ask for personal information such as your name, address, or phone number to use the Site. We may collect non-personally-identifiable information automatically through analytics and advertising partners, including your approximate location, browser type, device type, pages visited, and time spent on the Site.
privacy.section_cookies_paragraph: We use cookies and similar technologies to remember your language preference, understand how the Site is used, and serve advertising. You can disable cookies in your browser settings, although some features may not work as intended.
privacy.section_ads_paragraph_1: We use Google AdSense to display advertisements on this Site. Third-party vendors, including Google, use cookies to serve ads based on your prior visits to this and other websites.
privacy.section_ads_paragraph_2: Google's use of advertising cookies enables it and its partners to serve ads to you based on your visit to this Site and/or other sites on the Internet. You may opt out of personalized advertising by visiting Google's Ads Settings. For more information about how Google uses data, please review Google's Privacy & Terms.
privacy.section_analytics_paragraph: We may use analytics services to understand aggregate usage patterns. This data is used only to improve the Site and is not used to identify individual visitors.
privacy.section_thirdparty_paragraph: Our Site may contain links to external websites, such as Google Maps locations for the restaurants we recommend. We are not responsible for the privacy practices or content of those third-party sites.
privacy.section_children_paragraph: This Site is intended for a general audience and is not directed at children under the age of 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children.
privacy.section_changes_paragraph: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated revision date.
privacy.section_contact_paragraph: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at:

more_information.get_to_know_more: Get to know more about Thai cuisine
more_information.get_to_know_more_paragraph_1: Ever wonder why people completely fall head over heels for Thai food? Spoiler alert: it is not just about burning your tongue off with chilies! The real magic of Thai cuisine is how it brings total opposites together for a perfect collaboration. In just one single bite, you might get hit with sour, sweet, salty, spicy, and a tiny hint of bitter. When they all join forces, it creates an absolute flavor bomb.
more_information.get_to_know_more_paragraph_2: The secret weapon that makes everyone obsessed? The generous, unapologetic use of fresh herbs and spices. Think lemongrass, kaffir lime leaves, galangal, and fresh chilies turning a basic dish into a mind-blowing experience.
more_information.get_to_know_more_paragraph_3: Next time you order, try stepping out of your Pad Thai comfort zone. Get your spice on with a funky Som Tum (green papaya salad), slurp a bowl of rich Tom Saeb, or cozy up with a deeply flavorful Massaman curry. Trust me, once you enter the Thai food universe, there is no turning back. If you haven't tried these yet, go get some right now. Warning: you will definitely need a second plate of rice!
more_information.hidden_gems_paragraph_1: Okay, maybe they aren't that hidden, but hey, the Thai food scene is way more than just Jay Fai! Give some other places a shot and you'll realize there are tons of mind-blowingly good spots out there, even if tourists have never heard of them. Heck, some of them aren't even mainstream among locals because they don't bother with marketing. They survive purely on word-of-mouth.
more_information.hidden_gems_paragraph_2: Out of the zillion restaurant pins on Google Maps, the ones rocking a 4.3-star rating or higher with hundreds or thousands of reviews are usually a pretty safe bet for a great meal. We're talking about places like the seafood joints at the Ayutthaya Central Market that are just a little over an hour's drive from Bangkok, those killer suburban noodle shops less than an hour out of the city, or awesome Thai restaurants right in the Ari neighborhood that are just a quick BTS ride away.
more_information.recommendations: Places that totally won us over
more_information.recommendations_paragraph_1: We aren't getting paid a dime to say this, but as foodies who are pretty darn picky, we just want to give these spots the hype they deserve. That way they stay in business, and we get to keep eating there for a long, long time. Hehe.
